stemHtml: Which of the following statements is correct if one cell of an aircraft battery catches fire due to thermal runaway?

options.a: The fire will spread through the battery until the pilot turns off the battery switch, at which point the fire will stop burning.
options.b: The fire will spread to neighbouring cells and spread through the battery, destroying it.
options.c: The fire will be isolated in the cell in question and NOT spread further, although it might cause minor damage to neighbouring cells.
options.d: Aircraft batteries are constructed in such a way that they regenerate themselves after a thermal runaway, so any fire will be limited to small flames and is difficult to detect.
